title: hieracium cordatum subsp. citharocerinthe
intro: Hieracium cordatum subsp. citharocerinthe is a rare and lesser-known species of plant within the Hieracium genus, commonly referred to as a type of hawkweed. It has been found to possess medicinal properties due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Hieracium cordatum subsp. citharocerinthe include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with various respiratory conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas or infusions to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although its use is largely anecdotal and requires further research for validation.
